evidence.snippet:
### [SkyLink® 7100](https://naltec.com/products/skylink-7100/)
Provides continuous satellite voice and data connectivity for aviation and UAV operations…

From autonomous workboats to uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAV) to self-driving cars, our proven low size, weight, and power (SWaP) solutions enable continuous, highly-reliable connectivity and assured positioning, navigational, and timing (PNT) awareness, worldwide. Our beyond visual line of sight (BVLOS) communications capabilities offer effortless remote command and control (C2) of operations, fleets, and assets across land, sea, and air.

items.notes: Satellite comms terminals and data links explicitly for UAV/UAS operations; SkyLink 7100 targets aviation and UAV

products.notes: More products exist (catalog has 5+ pages); only page 1 fetched. Additional products include HawkEye 5500, Quicksilver, docking stations, satellite phones.

ndaa.notes: No NDAA Section 848/889 compliance claims found anywhere in the corpus. The compliance page (resource-0034) covers GDPR privacy policy only.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS or Blue sUAS list mentions found in any fetched page.

evidence.snippet:
Product Catalog
===============
Browse our industry-leading satellite and voice communications solutions and seamlessly connect your high-value assets and operations, anytime and anywhere.

9602-LP, 9602-A, 9602-AB, 9602-BTI, 9602-NAL
The 9602 satellite trackers by NAL Technologies are low size, weight, and power (SWaP) solutions leveraging Iridium’s Short Burst Data®  (SBD®) services to provide cost-effective connectivity for commercial and government customers.

readiness.notes: Multiple catalog SKUs with documented specs, variants, quick-start guides; ISO 9001 certified as of 2025

Vendor originally Blue Sky Network, rebranded as NAL Technologies (Naltec) in Jan 2026 after merger with NAL Research

tagline: Iridium-centric satellite modem, tracker, and PNT module supplier serving UAV command-and-control, aviation, and asset tracking via QuickNav, SkyLink, ALTM, and SHOUT product families. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of US defense adoption thin.
